paper_title: miRNA Analysis in Rice Pollen Development

methods.method_name: Small RNA Sequencing and Data Processing
methods.description: This method involves sequencing small RNAs from rice pollen and sporophytic tissues to investigate miRNA components and their dynamic changes during pollen development.

steps.description: Purified cells of UNMs, BCP, and TCP from rice and sequenced their small RNAs using Solexa high-throughput technology.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence small RNAs from rice pollen using Solexa technology

steps.description: Remove sequences of low quality, adaptor contaminants, and RNAs smaller than 18 nucleotides.
steps.searchable_summary: filter and clean small RNA sequences

steps.description: Map small RNAs of 18 to 30 nucleotides to the rice genome using SOAP.
steps.searchable_summary: map small RNAs to rice genome using SOAP

steps.description: Remove sequences corresponding to known non-coding RNAs and possibly degraded species of mRNAs.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out known non-coding RNAs and degraded mRNAs

steps.description: Identify candidate miRNAs by mapping to precursor sequences in the rice miRNA database.
steps.searchable_summary: identify candidate miRNAs using rice miRNA database

methods.description: This method predicts novel miRNAs by analyzing structural features of small RNAs and excluding those that do not meet specific criteria.

steps.description: Fold the flanking genome sequence of un-annotated small RNAs and analyze structural features to exclude those that cannot form the characteristic fold-back structure.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze structural features of small RNAs for fold-back structure

steps.description: Exclude small RNAs with multiple loci in the rice genome.
steps.searchable_summary: filter small RNAs with multiple loci

steps.description: Eliminate small RNAs of low abundance and those originating from both strands.
steps.searchable_summary: remove low abundance and dual-strand small RNAs

steps.description: Use 'MIREAP' to recover most kn-miRs and identify novel miRNA candidates.
steps.searchable_summary: identify novel miRNA candidates using MIREAP

steps.description: Blast precursor and mature sequences against the Oryza Repeat Database to discard homologs of repetitive sequences.
steps.searchable_summary: discard repetitive sequence homologs using Oryza Repeat Database

methods.description: This method predicts regulatory mRNA targets of identified miRNAs and analyzes their biological functions.

steps.description: Predict targets of all identified miRNAs using the psRNATarget web service.
Perform Gene Ontology (GO) abundance analysis of miRNA targets.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze GO terms of miRNA targets

steps.description: Compare expression profiles of miRNAs and their targets using transcriptome data.
steps.searchable_summary: compare miRNA and target expression profiles

methods.description: This method involves computational processing of small RNA sequencing data to identify and annotate small RNAs.

steps.description: Filter low quality reads and trim adaptor sequences using a modified dynamic programming algorithm.
Map sequences of 18 or more nucleotides to the rice genome using SOAP.
steps.searchable_summary: map sequences to rice genome using SOAP

steps.description: Discard sequences matching known rice rRNAs, tRNAs, snRNAs, and snoRNAs.
Identify and annotate repeat-associated small RNAs using a parallel overlap finding algorithm.
Identify kn-miRs by mapping sequences to miRNA precursors in the miRBase miRNA Database.
